title: Japan's Parliament approves reform of the Imperial House Law to ensure succession
summary: The new law allows the adoption of males to increase the number of heirs amid intense political debate and criticism from the opposition.

title: British Labour Party elects Burnham as new leader and next prime minister unopposed
summary: Burnham will assume the position of prime minister on July 20 after being selected without a vote at the party congress.

title: Agreement between Saga Prefecture and Japanese government to start environmental assessment of new Shinkansen section
summary: The agreement allows starting environmental impact studies for the extension of the Kyushu Shinkansen, with limits on the financial burden for Saga.

title: 37-year-old man arrested for abandoning the bodies of two women in Nara
summary: Police arrest the suspect after finding the lifeless bodies of a mother and her daughter on the riverbank in the prefecture.

title: Four new Living National Treasures designated by the Japanese government
summary: The Cultural Affairs Council proposes a nagauta performer, a goldsmith, and two other artists as Living National Treasures.

title: Japan's Diet extends its session by eight days until July 25
summary: The extension aims to pass key bills such as the Subcapital Headquarters law and other pending legislation.

title: Japan approves law penalizing destruction of the national flag
summary: The new legislation criminalizes the destruction of the flag and also penalizes live broadcasts that show it damaged.

title: Nikkei 225 index suffers a drop of 2,694 points, the fifth largest in history
summary: The Japanese stock market registers oversold signals and investors react with heavy selling during the session.

title: Japanese yen weakens to 162 per dollar, its lowest level in months
summary: The Japanese currency continues its decline amid the policy divergence between the Bank of Japan and the Federal Reserve.

title: Torrential rain alert in Kanto region with risk of flooding
summary: The Meteorological Agency issues warnings about the formation of intense rain lines in Tochigi and Gunma, and more storms are forecast.

title: Mass food poisoning at a Hachioji nursery affects 64 children
summary: A bacteria similar to E. coli is detected in food served at the nursery, causing gastrointestinal symptoms in dozens of children.

title: Japan Airlines implements preventive measures after crew alcohol consumption incident
summary: The airline reinforces controls to prevent flight attendants from consuming alcohol before flights after individual cases were detected.

title: Hiroshima Carp player Masaya Yano excluded for alleged relationship with traffickers
summary: The team decides to deregister the player after it was revealed that he met privately with individuals identified as drug dealers.

title: Voice actress Yukiko Nikaido dies at 87, known for her role in 'Lupin III'
summary: The seiyuu, wife of actor Hiroshi Yanagi, leaves an indelible legacy in Japanese animation.

title: Three candidates compete for the presidency of the Reiwa Shinsengumi party
summary: The candidates seek to overcome dependence on founder Taro Yamamoto and chart a new course for the party.

title: Verification committee finds gaps in Professor Mochizuki's proof of the ABC conjecture
summary: Experts issue an interim report stating that the mathematical proof still has unclear points that require clarification.

title: Class-action lawsuit against Kizuna Holding for sudden closure of employment centers for disabled people
summary: Former employees and users file a lawsuit for unjustified dismissal after the surprise closure of several assisted work facilities.

title: Traditional Gion Matsuri float parade takes place under extreme heat in Kyoto
summary: 23 floats parade through the city in the festival's main procession, facing temperatures above 38 degrees.

title: Body found in Kagoshima river may be that of missing 5-year-old boy
summary: The body is found 700 meters from the spa where the child disappeared, and authorities are conducting tests to confirm his identity.

title: Results of the first week of the Nagoya sumo tournament with several surprises
summary: Wrestler Shishi achieves six consecutive victories, while other contenders like Daieisho obtain notable results.
